close_reason: Fixed: DAGSTER_HOME was unset, causing dagster to hang looking for an instance. Set export DAGSTER_HOME=.dagster/ in Justfile. just sync now completes in ~7s with both assets running in parallel. Also added dagster-dg + dagster-components deps for dg CLI support.
description:
The 'just sync' recipe runs 'uv run dagster asset materialize --select * -m bugster.definitions'. It hangs indefinitely after printing startup warnings (SupersessionWarning, BetaWarning, XDG_DATA_HOME warning). No output and no exit.

Workaround: run the dlt pipeline directly in Python (see just sync-direct recipe if added).

Possible causes:
- Dagster tries to start a local instance/daemon and blocks
- dagster asset materialize is deprecated (warns to use 'dg launch --assets' instead, but 'dg' is not in PATH)

Options to investigate:
1. Install 'dg' (dagster-dg package) and switch just sync to use 'dg launch --assets *'
2. Add a just recipe that runs dlt pipelines directly via Python without Dagster CLI
3. Figure out why the Dagster CLI hangs (local instance timeout?)
title: just sync stalls via dagster asset materialize CLI

description:
Replace hand-rolled service adapters with dlt verified sources + custom @dlt.destination for TaskNotes.

Architecture:
  dlt source (github/jira/linear) → normalize → @dlt.destination → SyncEngine → markdown

Benefits:
- dlt-hub verified sources handle pagination, rate limits, incremental loading
- Custom destination: @dlt.destination maps table rows to ExternalItem + calls SyncEngine.sync()
- dagster-dlt integration wires sources into Dagster assets automatically
- Drop the IssueService layer entirely

Stack:
  pip install dlt dagster-dlt dlt[github] dlt[jira] dlt[linear]
title: Epic: dlt pipeline architecture

close_reason: Implemented @dlt.destination in bugster/destinations/tasknotes.py. _to_external_item() dispatches by table name (github_issues/jira_issues/linear_issues) with status+priority mapping per source. SyncEngine wiring unchanged. 11 new tests, 25 total passing.
description:
Implement the custom dlt destination that writes records to Obsidian TaskNote markdown files.

  @dlt.destination(batch_size=100, max_table_nesting=0)
  def tasknotes(items, table, vault_path=dlt.config.value, tasks_dir='00_Inbox/Tasks'):
      engine = SyncEngine(vault_path, tasks_dir)
      external_items = [_to_external_item(item, table['name']) for item in items]
      engine.sync(external_items)

Key work:
- _to_external_item() mapping per table name (github_issues, jira_issues, linear_issues)
- source_system, external_id, title, status, priority, upstream_modified from dlt record
- Wire into Dagster via dagster-dlt DltSource asset
- Remove IssueService abstraction layer (no longer needed)
- Tests: mock dlt pipeline run, assert TaskNotes created correctly

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/dlt-ecosystem/destinations/destination
title: Custom @dlt.destination for TaskNotes markdown

description:
The core differentiator from tnote: detect when a TaskNote's status changes locally and push that change back to the source system (e.g. close a GitHub issue when status → done/cancelled).

IssueService.push() and GitHubService.push() already exist. Need:
- Dagster asset or sensor that scans vault for changed TaskNotes
- Change detection: compare current status to upstream_modified timestamp or a state file
- Route each changed note to the correct service via source_system frontmatter field
- Handle: done/cancelled → close upstream, todo → reopen upstream
- Dry-run flag

Likely approach: git diff on vault (or mtime-based scan) to find recently modified .md files, then check if status frontmatter changed.
notes: Deferred: pull-only model for now (bugwarrior approach). Push path needs heavy testing before enabling — risk of accidentally closing upstream issues.
title: Push path: sync TaskNote changes back upstream

close_reason: Fixed: committed pyrightconfig.json + editable install via uv pip install -e .
description:
Pi's LSP uses a global Pyright binary that defaults to system Python (3.14 on this machine). When the project venv uses a different version (3.12), Pyright fails to find any installed packages — dagster, pytest, and the local package all show 'Import could not be resolved'.

FIX (already applied to this repo):
1. pyrightconfig.json at project root with venvPath+venv set — this is the reliable fix. pyproject.toml [tool.pyright] is often ignored by standalone Pyright binaries.
2. uv pip install -e . — editable install puts a .pth file in site-packages so Pyright finds the local package. uv sync does this automatically.

KEY: Do NOT add extraPaths pointing inside .venv — that duplicates what the venv setting already provides.

A global skill was created at ~/.pi/agent/skills/python-project-setup/SKILL.md to prevent this on future projects.

Session: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--Users-emiller-src-personal-bugster--/2026-02-21T17-34-38-355Z_fbf66322-8464-43e4-8b00-710f0507f943.jsonl
title: Pyright can't resolve imports when system Python != venv Python

title: definitions.py swallows bad-config errors silently

description:
Add Apple Reminders as a dlt source in Bugster, replacing tnote's `[integrations.reminders]` pull.

tnote config (gutted):
  lists = ["Reminders"]
  include_completed = false
  contexts = ["personal"]

Implementation approach: CalDAV VTODO
  Apple Reminders has synced via CalDAV (RFC 5545 VTODO) since forever —
  it's how third-party calendar apps access Reminders on non-Apple platforms.
  This is Apple's own standard iCloud sync protocol, not a hack.

  Endpoint: https://caldav.icloud.com
  Auth: app-specific password (iCloud account settings)
  Items: VTODO components inside VCALENDAR
  Libraries: caldav (PyPI), or raw requests/httpx

  Works identically on macOS and Linux — no platform-specific tooling needed.

  macOS-only alternative: remindctl (https://github.com/steipete/remindctl)
    brew install steipete/tap/remindctl && remindctl all --json
    macOS 14+ only — skip unless CalDAV auth becomes painful.

Implementation tasks:
- Use dlt @dlt.source pattern (see bugster/sources/github.py for reference)
- Map VTODO → ExternalItem with source_system = "reminders"
- Filter by list name (tnote used lists = ["Reminders"])
- Add [[reminders]] source type to BugsterConfig / config.py
- Wire into make_*_asset() factory pattern in assets/factory.py
notes:
Tool candidates:

macOS: remindctl (https://github.com/steipete/remindctl)
  - Swift CLI, macOS 14+ (Sonoma+), MIT
  - remindctl all --json → JSON array, perfect for dlt
  - install: brew install steipete/tap/remindctl

Linux: remindctl won't work (Swift + macOS Reminders framework)
  - Reminders syncs via iCloud CalDAV (VTODO items)
  - Endpoint: https://caldav.icloud.com
  - Options: vdirsyncer (sync CalDAV → local .ics), then parse VTODO
  - rclone iCloud = iCloud Drive only, NOT Reminders — dead end
  - Cleanest cross-platform path: CalDAV directly via requests/httpx
    (same approach works on both macOS and Linux, avoids remindctl dep)

Recommendation: implement as CalDAV VTODO source (platform-agnostic),
document remindctl as optional fast path on macOS if CalDAV auth is painful.
title: Apple Reminders source

description:
Add a bugwarriorrc-style TOML config file so users can define multiple named sources (e.g. personal GitHub, work GitHub, Jira). Currently hardwired to single-account EnvVar. Needed before real use.

Acceptance criteria:
- BUGSTER_CONFIG env var or ~/.config/bugster/config.toml default path
- [sources.github.personal] and [sources.github.work] style sections
- Each source section maps to a GitHubConfig (token, username, contexts, include_repos, etc.)
- Dagster definitions.py loads config and wires up one asset per named source
- Pydantic model validates the TOML on load with clear errors
- Support for future source types (jira, linear) in same schema
title: Config system: TOML multi-source config

description: Bugster-generated TaskNotes use status="open" and priority="normal" but vault lint requires status in {backlog,todo,in-progress,in-review,waiting,done,cancelled} and priority in {none,low,medium,high,urgent}. This blocks pre-commit (tnote task lint) on batch sync output.\n\nScope: fix source->TaskNote mapping so new sync output is valid for vault schema; avoid manual mass-edits.
notes: Started. Plan: add failing regression test for invalid TaskNote enums (open/normal), then fix mappings to emit vault-valid enums, then run targeted tests.
